issues.title: No skeleton loaders — blank pages flash on every data fetch
issues.description: Pages like Budget, RFIs, Submittals, and Dashboard render blank content while data loads. No skeleton screens exist matching content shape. Spinners are absent. A superintendent opening the app at 6am sees a blank white page for 1-3 seconds, destroying first impressions and trust.
issues.prompt:
Create a reusable skeleton loader system for SiteSyncAI.

File: src/components/ui/Skeletons.tsx

Implement the following exports:
- `MetricCardSkeleton`: 5 side-by-side skeleton cards, 120px tall, matching MetricCard shape
- `TableRowSkeleton({ rows = 8 })`: renders N skeleton rows at 48px height with 4 column placeholders
- `GanttSkeleton`: 3 stacked horizontal bars of varying widths
- `ChatMessageSkeleton`: avatar circle + 2 text lines
- All skeletons use `bg-gray-200 animate-pulse rounded` with `opacity-60`

File: src/styles/tokens.css
Add: `@keyframes pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 0.3; } 50% { opacity: 0.7; } }` with 1.5s cycle.

Integrate into: src/pages/Budget.tsx, src/pages/RFIs.tsx, src/pages/Dashboard.tsx, src/pages/Submittals.tsx
Wrap each page's data-dependent render block: `if (isLoading) return <MetricCardSkeleton />` at the top section, `<TableRowSkeleton rows={8} />` below.

Acceptance criteria:
- Zero blank-page flashes on any page load
- Skeleton shape matches rendered content within 10% width/height
- Pulse animation runs at exactly 1.5s cycle
- No spinners anywhere in the app

issues.title: Missing ARIA roles and keyboard navigation across all data tables
issues.description: src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx and VirtualDataTable.tsx have no role='grid', aria-rowcount, aria-rowindex, or aria-selected attributes. Table headers lack scope='col'. Focus management is absent — pressing Tab skips rows entirely. This fails WCAG 2.1 AA SC 1.3.1 and 4.1.2, blocking any enterprise procurement with accessibility requirements.
issues.prompt:
Audit and fix accessibility in src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx and src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx.

Changes required:
1. Add `role='grid'` to the outer table element
2. Add `role='columnheader' scope='col'` to all `<th>` elements
3. Add `role='row'` to each `<tr>`, `aria-rowindex={index + 1}`, `aria-selected={isSelected}`
4. Add `role='gridcell'` to each `<td>`
5. Add `aria-rowcount={totalRows}` to the `<table>` element
6. Implement keyboard navigation in src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts (file exists — verify it is actually wired to DataTable via `onKeyDown` handler on the grid container)
7. Add `tabIndex={0}` to the grid container and `tabIndex={-1}` to each row, set `tabIndex={0}` only on the focused row
8. On Enter key, call the row's `onClick` handler
9. Add visible focus ring: `focus:outline-2 focus:outline-orange-500 focus:outline-offset-0`

File: src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts
Ensure the hook returns `{ focusedIndex, handleKeyDown }` and is exported and consumed by DataTable.

Acceptance criteria:
- axe-core reports zero violations on DataTable component
- Tab moves focus into grid, J/K or arrow keys navigate rows
- Enter opens the focused row detail
- Screen reader announces row count and position

issues.title: aiHealthScore hardcoded to 85 in projects endpoint — CFO cannot trust any metric
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/projects.ts line ~getMetrics, `aiHealthScore: 85` and `aiConfidenceLevel: 74` are hardcoded constants. Every project shows identical health scores regardless of actual schedule variance, budget status, or RFI overdue count. This is the exact metric a CFO and GC executive rely on for portfolio decisions.

issues.title: Empty states show nothing — no illustration, no CTA, no helpful message
issues.description: When RFI, Submittal, Punch List, and Daily Log tables return zero rows, pages render an empty table body with no empty state UI. Per design standards and founder priorities, every empty state must show a specific message, relevant icon, and a primary action button. A superintendent seeing a blank table will assume the app is broken.
issues.prompt:
Create a reusable EmptyState component and wire it into all list pages.

File: src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx
```tsx
interface EmptyStateProps {
  icon: React.ReactNode
  title: string
  description: string
  action?: { label: string; onClick: () => void }
}
```
Layout: centered column, icon at 48px in a light gray circle (bg-gray-100 rounded-full p-3), title in 16px/600, description in 14px/400 muted, primary orange button below.

Wire into the following pages with page-specific copy:
- src/pages/RFIs.tsx: icon=FileQuestion, title='No RFIs yet', description='RFIs track design questions and field conflicts. Create your first to get started.', action='Create RFI'
- src/pages/Submittals.tsx: icon=ClipboardList, title='No submittals on file', description='Submittals track shop drawings and product data approvals.', action='Create Submittal'
- src/pages/PunchList.tsx: icon=CheckSquare, title='Punch list is clear', description='All items resolved. New punch items appear here during inspections.', action='Add Item'
- src/pages/DailyLog.tsx: icon=BookOpen, title='No logs this week', description='Daily logs are your legal record of site activity. Start today.', action='Log Today'

Pattern: in each page's render, check `data.length === 0 && !isLoading` and render `<EmptyState ...>`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Zero pages show an empty table body without an EmptyState
- Each EmptyState has a unique, domain-specific title and description
- CTA button opens the relevant create modal

issues.title: Widespread use of `as any` casts suppresses type safety across API layer
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/organizations.ts uses `as any[]` on line mapping organization_members rows. src/api/endpoints/projectMembers.ts uses `as any` on insert/update calls bypassing table type constraints. src/api/endpoints/projects.ts casts with no validation. These casts allow malformed data through silently, risk runtime crashes, and block TypeScript's value in catching bugs early.
issues.prompt:
Eliminate `as any` casts from the API endpoints layer.

File: src/api/endpoints/organizations.ts
- Replace `((data ?? []) as any[]).map((row) => row.organization)` with a properly typed join. Create interface `OrgMemberRow { organization: Organization }` and cast `data` as `OrgMemberRow[] | null`.

File: src/api/endpoints/projectMembers.ts
- The `(supabase.from('project_members') as any)` pattern exists because the generated types may not include insert return types. Fix by running `supabase gen types typescript` and ensuring `project_members` insert/update types are present in src/types/database.ts. Then remove the `as any` wrapper.

File: src/types/api.ts
- Add strict interfaces for every row type that currently uses implicit `any`: `BudgetItemRow`, `ChangeOrderRow`, `RfiRow`, `SubmittalRow`, `SchedulePhaseRow`, `DailyLogRow`, `PunchItemRow`, `CrewRow`, `DirectoryContactRow`, `MeetingRow`, `DrawingRow`, `FileRow`, `TaskRow`.

File: tsconfig.json
- Set `"strict": true`, `"noImplicitAny": true`, `"strictNullChecks": true` if not already set.

Acceptance criteria:
- `tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors after changes
- Zero `as any` casts in src/api/endpoints/*.ts
- All row types in src/types/api.ts have explicit field definitions

issues.title: Command palette (Cmd+K) exists in code but keyboard shortcut system is not wired app-wide
issues.description: src/components/shared/CommandPalette.tsx exists and src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts exists, but there is no global registration of shortcuts in src/App.tsx or a root layout. G+D, G+R, G+B navigation shortcuts, N for new item, and J/K for table navigation are defined in design standards but not enforced. A PM who learns one keyboard shortcut and it fails will lose trust in the product.
issues.prompt:
Wire the keyboard shortcut system globally in SiteSyncAI.

File: src/App.tsx
Import `useKeyboardShortcuts` and register all global shortcuts inside the authenticated route shell:
```ts
useKeyboardShortcuts([
  { keys: ['meta+k'], action: () => openCommandPalette() },
  { keys: ['meta+/'], action: () => openShortcutOverlay() },
  { keys: ['g', 'd'], sequential: true, action: () => navigate('/dashboard') },
  { keys: ['g', 'r'], sequential: true, action: () => navigate('/rfis') },
  { keys: ['g', 'b'], sequential: true, action: () => navigate('/budget') },
  { keys: ['g', 's'], sequential: true, action: () => navigate('/schedule') },
  { keys: ['escape'], action: () => closeAllModals() },
])
```

File: src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts
Ensure the hook:
1. Supports sequential key chords (g then d within 1000ms)
2. Does not fire when focus is inside an input, textarea, or contenteditable
3. Accepts a `sequential` flag for two-key navigation shortcuts

File: src/components/ui/ShortcutOverlay.tsx (already exists)
Verify it renders all registered shortcuts in a two-column grid with key badge + description.

File: src/components/shared/CommandPalette.tsx
Ensure it opens on `meta+k`, has a search input auto-focused, and lists navigation + action shortcuts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cmd+K opens command palette from any page
- G then D navigates to dashboard within 1 second of second keypress
- Shortcut does not fire when typing in a form input
- Cmd+/ opens shortcut overlay listing all registered shortcuts

issues.title: Touch targets below 44px minimum on metric cards and table rows on mobile
issues.description: Design standards require 44px minimum touch targets per Apple HIG. Table rows are set to 48px on desktop but CSS does not enforce this on mobile breakpoints. Metric cards have padding that collapses below 44px height on screens under 375px. A superintendent with gloves cannot reliably tap rows, creating the exact friction that kills field adoption.
issues.prompt:
Enforce 44px minimum touch targets across all interactive elements on mobile.

File: src/index.css or src/styles/tokens.css
Add global rule:
```css
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  button, a, [role='button'], [role='row'], [role='gridcell'] {
    min-height: 44px;
    min-width: 44px;
  }
}
```

File: src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx
Ensure `<tr>` elements have `className='h-12 min-h-[44px]'` (48px = 3rem, above the 44px minimum).

File: src/components/Primitives.tsx (or wherever MetricCard is defined)
Ensure metric card root element has `min-h-[80px]` on mobile and `p-4` minimum padding so the tap area is never below 44px.

File: src/components/layout/MobileLayout.tsx
Audit all interactive children for touch target compliance. Add `py-3` (24px top+bottom = 48px for 14px text elements) to any nav items, tab buttons, or action buttons that lack explicit height.

File: src/components/mobile/MobileTaskCards.tsx
Ensure each card has `min-h-[64px]` and full-width tap area.

Run: Add a Playwright mobile test that checks `getBoundingClientRect().height >= 44` for the first 5 interactive elements on /rfis, /daily-log, and /dashboard on iPhone 13 viewport.

Acceptance criteria:
- Zero interactive elements below 44px height on mobile viewport
- No regression in desktop layout
- Playwright test passes on iPhone 13 viewport (390x844)

issues.title: Color token system defined in theme.ts but not enforced — direct Tailwind color classes bypass the design system
issues.description: src/styles/theme.ts and src/styles/tokens.css define the SiteSyncAI color palette (#F47820 orange, #4EC896 teal, #0F1629 sidebar navy) but components use raw Tailwind classes like `bg-orange-500`, `text-gray-600`, `bg-blue-100` that do not map to the design token values. This creates visual inconsistency — the orange used in buttons does not match the orange in metric card trends.
issues.prompt:
Enforce the SiteSyncAI design token system by extending Tailwind config.

File: tailwind.config.ts
Add to `theme.extend.colors`:
```ts
colors: {
  brand: {
    orange: '#F47820',
    navy: '#0F1629',
    teal: '#4EC896',
    amber: '#F5A623',
    danger: '#E74C3C',
    info: '#3B82F6',
  },
  surface: {
    page: '#F7F8FA',
    card: '#FFFFFF',
    sidebar: '#0F1629',
  },
  text: {
    primary: '#1A1A2E',
    muted: '#6B7280',
    disabled: '#9CA3AF',
  },
  border: {
    DEFAULT: '#E5E7EB',
  },
}
```

File: src/styles/tokens.css
Ensure CSS variables match: `--color-brand-orange: #F47820` etc.

Then do a project-wide find-and-replace for the most common violations:
- `bg-orange-500` → `bg-brand-orange`
- `text-orange-500` → `text-brand-orange`
- `bg-blue-500` → `bg-brand-info`
- `text-red-500` → `text-brand-danger`
- `bg-green-500` → `bg-brand-teal`

Update src/components/Primitives.tsx, src/components/Sidebar.tsx, src/components/TopBar.tsx, src/pages/Dashboard.tsx as priority files.

Acceptance criteria:
- No raw Tailwind color classes for primary/danger/success/info in Primitives.tsx or Sidebar.tsx
- Visual regression test shows no color shift on Dashboard, RFIs, Budget pages
- All brand colors match hex values in design spec exactly

issues.title: VirtualDataTable exists but large list pages (RFIs, Submittals) use the non-virtual DataTable
issues.description: src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx implements virtual scrolling but src/pages/RFIs.tsx and src/pages/Submittals.tsx import the standard DataTable. Projects with 500+ RFIs (common on large commercial jobs) will render all rows to the DOM simultaneously, causing scroll jank and memory pressure on iPhone. Per design standards, virtual scrolling is required for 50+ rows.
issues.prompt:
Replace DataTable with VirtualDataTable on all list pages that can exceed 50 rows.

File: src/pages/RFIs.tsx
- Replace `import { DataTable } from '../components/shared/DataTable'` with `import { VirtualDataTable } from '../components/shared/VirtualDataTable'`
- Ensure VirtualDataTable receives: `data={rfis}`, `columns={columns}`, `rowHeight={48}`, `containerHeight={600}`, `onRowClick={(row) => navigate(`/rfis/${row.id}`)}`

File: src/pages/Submittals.tsx
- Same replacement pattern

File: src/pages/PunchList.tsx
- Same replacement pattern

File: src/components/shared/VirtualDataTable.tsx
Audit the component:
1. Ensure it uses `react-window` or a manual overscan approach
2. Ensure it supports `onRowClick`, `selectedRows`, `onSelectionChange` props matching DataTable's API
3. Ensure sticky column headers work correctly with virtual scroll
4. Ensure keyboard navigation (J/K) updates the virtual scroll offset

If VirtualDataTable API diverges from DataTable, add an adapter interface in src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx that re-exports a unified `<Table>` component choosing the implementation based on `data.length > 50`.

Acceptance criteria:
- RFI list with 500 rows renders in under 100ms
- Only visible rows + overscan (10 above/below) are in the DOM at any time
- Row click navigation works identically to DataTable
- No layout shift on scroll

issues.title: auditTrail.ts throws raw Supabase error objects instead of typed ApiError
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts calls `throw error` directly on Supabase errors instead of `throw transformSupabaseError(error)`. This bypasses the typed error system established in src/api/errors.ts, sends raw Supabase PostgREST error objects to the UI, and may expose internal database structure in error messages — a security and UX concern for enterprise customers.
issues.prompt:
Fix error handling in src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts to use the typed ApiError system.

File: src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts

1. Add import at top:
```ts
import { transformSupabaseError } from '../client'
```

2. In `getAuditLog`, replace:
```ts
if (error) throw error
```
with:
```ts
if (error) throw transformSupabaseError(error)
```

3. In `getEntityHistory`, same replacement.

4. Verify src/api/errors.ts `transformSupabaseError` function:
- Accepts `{ message: string; code?: string; details?: string | null }`
- Returns an `ApiError` instance with `message`, `code`, `statusCode`
- Does NOT include raw `details` that might contain SQL schema information in the exposed `message` field
- Logs the full error internally (to Sentry/console) but exposes only a safe user-facing message

5. Update src/pages/AuditTrail.tsx error boundary / error display to render the `ApiError.message` string only, never the raw error object.

Acceptance criteria:
- Zero raw `throw error` statements in auditTrail.ts
- Error caught in UI shows 'Failed to load audit log' not a PostgREST error string
- src/test/api/errors.test.ts has a test case for auditTrail error transformation
- No database table names or SQL appear in any user-facing error message

invented_features.title: Ambient Field Status Bar
invented_features.description: A persistent 8px color-coded bar across the top of every mobile screen that shows real-time site health: green (all clear), amber (RFIs overdue or weather warning), red (safety incident or critical budget breach). A superintendent with gloves can glance at the bar color without reading any text. Pairs with haptic feedback — one pulse for amber, three for red.

invented_features.title: Design Token Audit Bot
invented_features.description: A CI step that scans all TSX files for raw Tailwind color classes that violate the design token system (e.g., bg-orange-500 instead of bg-brand-orange) and fails the build with a lint error listing each violation file and line. Keeps the design system honest as the codebase grows.

invented_features.title: Metric Card Drill-Down Drawer
invented_features.description: Every metric card on every page is clickable and opens a slide-in drawer showing the full calculation breakdown: which line items compose the number, trend over the last 30 days, and an AI sentence explaining the trend in plain English. A CFO can click 'Committed Cost' and see every subcontract and PO that makes up the number, traceable to the source record.

competitive_intel: The design system foundation is architecturally sound — token files, theme constants, and component primitives exist — but the gap between definition and enforcement is critical. Procore's UI is notoriously dense and slow, giving SiteSyncAI a real opportunity, but only if the polish gap is closed. Currently, hardcoded metrics (aiHealthScore: 85), missing skeleton loaders, and absent empty states would embarrass the product in a live demo against Procore's polished (if slow) interface. Fieldwire beats SiteSyncAI on mobile touch target compliance today. Linear and Stripe — the stated benchmark — have zero tolerance for raw error objects surfacing to users or color inconsistency across components. The keyboard shortcut system (a Linear differentiator) is architecturally present but functionally broken. Closing these 10 issues would move SiteSyncAI from 'impressive prototype' to 'credible enterprise product' in the specific areas — mobile field UX, CFO trust in metrics, and PM keyboard efficiency — where the competitive win is decided.
summary: The SiteSyncAI UI Design System has strong architectural bones: a token system, theme constants, typed API helpers, skeleton and empty state components exist in the file tree, and the component library covers every major construction workflow. However, the gap between what exists as a file and what is actually wired and enforced is the defining problem of this audit. Three critical failures stand out: hardcoded health scores (85, 74) that would be immediately spotted by any CFO running a real project, absent skeleton loaders causing blank-page flashes on every page load, and empty states that never render — leaving supers staring at empty table bodies. Accessibility is the weakest dimension: DataTable lacks ARIA grid roles, keyboard navigation is defined but not globally registered, and touch targets fall below Apple's 44px minimum on mobile. TypeScript quality is undermined by `as any` casts in the organizations and projectMembers endpoints that defeat the type safety the codebase otherwise invests in. Color consistency is aspirational — tokens are defined but raw Tailwind classes bypass them in production components. The performance investment in VirtualDataTable exists but is not used where it matters most. Fixing the 10 issues identified here — particularly the hardcoded metrics, missing skeletons, empty states, and keyboard shortcut wiring — would move the overall score from 62 to approximately 78 and make SiteSyncAI credibly competitive against Procore in a head-to-head enterprise evaluation.
